f12b27fccdd0bd5c542d32e8fb5c7633971a3fe1.title: Multi-agent CLI compatibility and version bump to v3.5.0
f12b27fccdd0bd5c542d32e8fb5c7633971a3fe1.rationale: The core router must achieve wider compatibility with multiple agent/plugin environments (Claude, Codex, Factory) to standardize its API and integration points, ensuring consistent LLM routing regardless of the initiating platform. This massive expansion increases the system's reach but also significantly increases integration complexity.

f9d1edf8a93947da8f5df26aebeab2c31999ee03.title: agent-context chain reordering
f9d1edf8a93947da8f5df26aebeab2c31999ee03.rationale: The system was updated to make the model chain aware of the active agent context (e.g., Claude Code or Codex). By reordering the chain, the application maximizes the use of models covered by existing paid subscriptions before defaulting to potentially expensive pay-per-call APIs, optimizing both cost and perceived value for the user.

5b3fd668a66f2dbe2253cd7b30b6d1a7b2b6aae7.title: Implement Codex plugin integration for LLM Router
5b3fd668a66f2dbe2253cd7b30b6d1a7b2b6aae7.rationale: The primary goal was to make the LLM Router a first-class, discoverable plugin within the Codex ecosystem. This integration shifts the deployment model from local package installation to marketplace discovery, significantly expanding the tool's reach and usability for developers using Codex.

161e2656086f2f2040c48c32a944c3634aae7137.title: Fix: Flush JSONL savings log before calculating cumulative usage totals
161e2656086f2f2040c48c32a944c3634aae7137.rationale: The previous flow caused a 'one-session lag' in calculating cumulative savings for free-provider usage. The fix requires synchronously importing pending raw savings records (JSONL) into the statistical table before the cumulative query runs, ensuring accurate and timely period totals across all providers.

7cadfd29776b72bb9b4491f128262d81eaef7ac1.title: Implement structured Policy Engine, Savings Digest, and Community Benchmarks
7cadfd29776b72bb9b4491f128262d81eaef7ac1.rationale: To move the LLM router from a simple mechanism to a governed, enterprise-grade tool. The Policy Engine provides necessary governance (precedence, allow/deny lists, cost capping); the Savings Digest provides financial transparency (spend spike detection, reporting); and the Community Benchmarks enable data-driven model optimization based on observed performance.

d595ba3cf1ec0120451a92c37c5e9724ff58edbc.title: Architecting Multi-Host Support and Refactoring Core Cost/Usage Logic
d595ba3cf1ec0120451a92c37c5e9724ff58edbc.rationale: The primary goal is to enable tracking of usage and savings across multiple host environments (Codex, Claude Desktop, Copilot) and to ensure persistent, cross-session cost accounting. This required fixing the gap in cross-session savings data logging.
d595ba3cf1ec0120451a92c37c5e9724ff58edbc.rejected: 1. Full Phase 1 'core refactor' is deemed unnecessary due to existing infrastructure. 2. Codex CLI does not expose a suitable hook API, accepting a capability extension fallback. 3. The output format for llm_auto will be a formatted string, not JSON, simplifying implementation.

abb2109e003df02b4684f723c50db79bc8f2f7d5.title: Implement llm_auto wrapper for persistent savings tracking
abb2109e003df02b4684f723c50db79bc8f2f7d5.rationale: The standard usage hooks (JSONL) can be unreliable or unavailable when integrating with external clients (e.g., Codex CLI, Claude Desktop) that lack a standard UserPromptSubmit hook. This change introduces `llm_auto`, which forces the import of local savings records (`import_savings_log`) at critical points (routing, usage summary, savings report) to ensure that cost and usage tracking is persistent and accurate regardless of which client or host triggered the call. This centralizes cost calculation logic.

bc9cd2e511af1ea5fa2ece04e9440f13185e344d.title: Improve cost data fidelity by adding source host attribution and enhancing usage projection logic
bc9cd2e511af1ea5fa2ece04e9440f13185e344d.rationale: The addition of the 'host' column ensures better attribution and tracking of cost savings sources across different systems/call origins. Furthermore, updating the cost projection logic to prioritize a 30-day monthly average (over weekly/daily) improves the reliability and stability of forecasted resource costs.

0ff67357f156eaea0622c15a7995ff2eae971395.title: Block local file reading tools (Glob/Read/Grep/LS) for Q&A tasks in hard enforcement mode
0ff67357f156eaea0622c15a7995ff2eae971395.rationale: The previous architecture allowed the LLM to read local files (Glob, Read, Grep, LS) directly, which the team determined was functionally equivalent to the LLM self-answering, bypassing necessary controls. By introducing task-specific blocklisting, file reading is only permitted for code tasks, forcing Q&A content through explicit, controlled analysis pipelines (llm_analyze/llm_query).

03ab0263cc7d1deb7d1540b08d9be6aaa4fce69e.title: Implement Team Dashboard and Multi-Channel Reporting
03ab0263cc7d1deb7d1540b08d9be6aaa4fce69e.rationale: The decision was made to treat the LLM router as a managed resource for an entire team, not just an individual. This centralizes identity resolution (user/project ID), aggregates usage costs/savings, and automates communication of usage reports to various team channels (Slack, Discord, Telegram). This provides critical visibility needed for enterprise adoption and cost management.

13ac6ca729dda1737436d916764bf625c49b68b8.title: Always prioritize local Ollama inference in the routing chain
13ac6ca729dda1737436d916764bf625c49b68b8.rationale: Previously, local Ollama models were only injected conditionally (e.g., in BUDGET profiles or when paid API quota pressure was high). The decision changed this to *always* inject Ollama when configured because local models are free and do not consume quotas. This increases the reliability of the fallback chain, ensuring a local attempt is made for virtually all tasks, improving resilience and local-first operations.

2ab3c6a0d08419dc8393a1faf9b2ee59221a1485.title: Implement Latency-Aware User Acceptance Scoring in Router
2ab3c6a0d08419dc8393a1faf9b2ee59221a1485.rationale: The system is moving beyond simple cost or raw latency metrics. By incorporating a user-acceptance feedback loop (calculating acceptance scores from `was_good` flags), the routing engine can automatically penalize or deprioritize models that, while fast, fail to meet user expectations, improving the overall quality and utility of the suggestions.

153c5cedb28000e306051237094d88221867bdba.title: Change Default Enforcement Mode to 'Smart' for Improved UX
153c5cedb28000e306051237094d88221867bdba.rationale: The previous default of 'hard' enforcement was too restrictive, blocking developer workflows for code tasks. The 'smart' mode default is adopted to provide maximum savings/guidance for Q&A tasks (query/research/generate/analyze) while maintaining a soft enforcement approach for code tasks, significantly improving the developer workflow experience without sacrificing core routing goals.

df21d97f68eb34a3af6f21ac1b699df775db4e13.title: Fix Stale State Cleanup on Session Start
df21d97f68eb34a3af6f21ac1b699df775db4e13.rationale: A state management bug was addressed where forcefully terminating a Claude session would leave behind orphaned temporary state files (`pending_route_*.json`), which would prevent the start of subsequent sessions. This cleanup ensures robust session continuity.

d7e08e4aa506290c5c77a713978480e01f0b7bcf.title: Context-Aware Routing: Continuation Prompt State Inheritance
d7e08e4aa506290c5c77a713978480e01f0b7bcf.rationale: To significantly improve performance and reduce latency during conversational flows, the system was updated to detect simple continuation prompts (e.g., 'ok', 'yes', 'stop'). Instead of running the full, expensive classification chain for these inputs, the system now inherits the prior turn's task context, allowing the router to process the input instantly and efficiently.

f8f2410179d76c4c11e722633cc0ec149ca19cb3.title: Implement repo-aware, layered configuration system
f8f2410179d76c4c11e722633cc0ec149ca19cb3.rationale: The core decision is to move configuration management from a single source to a hierarchical, 'effective' system. This allows the system to respect project-specific settings (.llm-router.yml) while enabling necessary global overrides (user routing.yaml) and maintaining strict type checking/linting, dramatically improving reliability and usability for multi-project environments.

c8f3b80b9693a7df2ddd6a2171fa251cf0775607.title: Implement Comprehensive Onboarding Wizard and Operational Mode Selection
c8f3b80b9693a7df2ddd6a2171fa251cf0775607.rationale: The routing system architecture requires a standardized, zero-friction setup. This decision formalizes the initialization process by detecting available backends (local, paid APIs), recommending a profile, and, critically, allowing the user to select a required operational enforcement mode (shadow, suggest, enforce). This stabilizes the system's entry point and initial configuration.

336375d9d3284d501c1aac924214756faf973c50.title: Implement cumulative savings reporting (daily/weekly/monthly) in session-end hook
336375d9d3284d501c1aac924214756faf973c50.rationale: To improve user transparency and demonstrate the tangible value proposition of the service. By quantifying the cost savings against a predefined baseline (Sonnet cost) across various time frames, the system provides stronger justification for its use.

574f0dd06cbd910572bf33f4d33db26714fa1bbd.title: Improve robustness and state reliability of session hooks
574f0dd06cbd910572bf33f4d33db26714fa1bbd.rationale: To ensure the usage tracking mechanism maintains accurate state and billing data. This required implementing two key enhancements: 1) Writing a fresh snapshot of current usage metrics upon session end, making the state less dependent on the next session's start hook. 2) Introducing robust, multi-source .env loading to guarantee that necessary configuration variables are available when hooks run in potentially limited environments.

d1e880d949eaf6c9bdfb97563f7dbe2e091330d9.title: Implement MCP-aware routing bypass
d1e880d949eaf6c9bdfb97563f7dbe2e091330d9.rationale: The implementation introduces pattern-matching logic to bypass standard LLM routing directives when the incoming user prompt explicitly matches a known, high-confidence external MCP (e.g., GitHub, Notion, Calendar). This makes the router more robust, more efficient, and reduces unnecessary context complexity for specialized service interactions.

9c3bbf326e7df98fddf9130bc4a3c3bbd1aa8f66.title: Explainable Routing and Cost Transparency (v2.2.0)
9c3bbf326e7df98fddf9130bc4a3c3bbd1aa8f66.rationale: To transform LLM usage from a 'black box' cost center into a transparent, cost-aware utility. By providing real-time explanations (cost prefixes, model comparisons), the system guides the user toward optimal, cheaper model selection at the point of decision.

b9ca201be64233f0b84cea973f6d76a3f6734b12.title: Implement Routing Simulation and Savings Dashboard for Building Trust
b9ca201be64233f0b84cea973f6d76a3f6734b12.rationale: The primary architectural goal was moving the product from merely being a router to being a trusted cost-management tool. By introducing a Route Simulator and a Savings Dashboard, the development team aimed to address the 'adoption blocker,' which is user trust. The system now quantifies value (cost savings, efficiency multiplier 'Nx') to prove its utility before the user fully commits to the optimized routing.

a999431cf866f57c2046f6e5b637c3d3cbd2074a.title: Implement LLM Route Simulator and Cost Analysis Dashboard
a999431cf866f57c2046f6e5b637c3d3cbd2074a.rationale: To provide users with a transparent, pre-run cost estimation and performance simulation dashboard. This feature is critical for adoption and understanding the value proposition of the automated LLM routing and cost optimization features, preventing unexpected usage costs.

612597d8aca757a5b10f4f3062e4f6e760ecc51a.title: Improve robustness by hiding provider health segment when status data is missing or stale
612597d8aca757a5b10f4f3062e4f6e760ecc51a.rationale: Previously, the status bar would display stale or default 'unknown' health values if the health tracking file (health.json) was missing, corrupted, or too old. This change updates the reading logic to return 'None' under these failure conditions, causing the entire health segment to be gracefully hidden, thus preventing the UI from misleading the user with inaccurate data.

87fb31601084ec6e8042e7397443b8c86d4da314.title: Strategic Product Roadmap: Phased adoption prioritizing Trust and Explainability
87fb31601084ec6e8042e7397443b8c86d4da314.rationale: The development strategy pivots to address user trust as the primary adoption blocker, rather than solely focusing on cost savings. The rollout is gated into phases (Trust & Proof -> Smarter Routing) to ensure foundational visibility and user confidence are established before advanced features are implemented.
87fb31601084ec6e8042e7397443b8c86d4da314.rejected: A development focus solely on cost-optimization without first addressing the visibility and trust surrounding the model routing decisions.

08aa7dd243bfd145f049c6d63e7ffd298eca98c5.title: Harden and enforce structured routing in Claude Code hooks
08aa7dd243bfd145f049c6d63e7ffd298eca98c5.rationale: The primary goal is to enforce a stricter contract for how the LLM interacts with the tools, preventing the model from arbitrarily jumping to tools (like Bash/Write) without the necessary routing signals. This hard-by-default enforcement improves system reliability and data integrity by making the LLM's output flow more predictable. It also introduces logging to detect and alert users about missed or incomplete routing turns, improving debugging and observability.

0f7fb559d762b5fe6241b317a9f5a4ec528deca8.title: Implement 'claw-code' mode for mandatory local inference routing
0f7fb559d762b5fe6241b317a9f5a4ec528deca8.rationale: The previous routing logic only prioritized local inference (Ollama) when running in 'BUDGET' profiles or when hitting high cloud quota pressure. This change introduces a 'claw-code' mode, recognizing that when running inside open-source alternatives to paid APIs (like claw-code), *every* API call is paid. Therefore, the decision mandates that Ollama must always be injected and attempted first, regardless of the profile or quota status, to minimize operational costs.

0ef50c18f8fa54f87aaa04aeeb49bd9ff3969cae.title: Implement Agno integration via RouteredModel and RouteredTeam
0ef50c18f8fa54f87aaa04aeeb49bd9ff3969cae.rationale: To solve the problem of scalable, cost-optimized multi-agent LLM usage. By implementing RouteredModel, agent calls can automatically be routed to the cheapest capable LLM provider (e.g., Ollama -> Codex -> paid APIs) based on complexity. RouteredTeam provides centralized, automated budget enforcement for multi-agent workflows.

a9c57515fd341b371d510c2eeeb03edabeb455a8.title: Improved API Error Handling: Content Filtering & Model Subscription Compliance
a9c57515fd341b371d510c2eeeb03edabeb455a8.rationale: Previously, content filter errors (policy blocks) were treated as hard infrastructure failures, causing unnecessary circuit breaker trips. This change treats them as soft errors, allowing the router to silently skip them and continue attempting the next model. Additionally, explicit model overrides now respect subscription mode, ensuring the LLM router never calls a non-subscribed model while operating in restricted mode.

da40c8743ff84c499add04bc583b0e48505d1f5c.title: Active API Key Purging for Subscription Mode
da40c8743ff84c499add04bc583b0e48505d1f5c.rationale: Implemented active purging of the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the runtime environment on every configuration lookup. This ensures that the key is fresh and secure, addressing the issue where long-lived processes or external environment setups could cause the key to persist and leak to the LLM router, even after previous hotfixes were applied.

db901220759c0dc8cdb3cf7d04ea4e7c2da29793.title: Block ANTHROPIC_API_KEY export in subscription mode
db901220759c0dc8cdb3cf7d04ea4e7c2da29793.rationale: The previous logic unconditionally exported the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, meaning that even when running in a controlled Claude Code subscription environment, auxiliary code paths could still make direct Anthropic API calls. This fix implements a hard guarantee by skipping key export when in subscription mode, preventing unexpected separate billing and maintaining the integrity of the billing model.

7abd3a655dbc127c26d1d368b14846cea6d4169d.title: Improve Codex agent detection mechanism
7abd3a655dbc127c26d1d368b14846cea6d4169d.rationale: The agent selection logic was previously flawed, relying on `is_codex_plugin_available()` which only checked for a specific plugin directory. This change correctly switches to using `is_codex_available()` to check for the actual Codex CLI binary path. This ensures better compatibility and detection accuracy for users who install Codex via global CLI tools.

0cb3cb0bd08cef80cd51515df9ae473e6b3a0414.title: Overhaul Model Routing Strategy: From Direct Directives to Free-First Tool Chains
0cb3cb0bd08cef80cd51515df9ae473e6b3a0414.rationale: The previous model routing strategy relied on deprecated and unreliable '/model' directives, which were found to be functionally broken or ignored by the underlying Claude Code model in context. The new architecture mandates that all routing (simple, moderate, complex) goes through a structured, 'free-first' Multi-Chain Processor (MCP) tool chain. This approach ensures cost efficiency and reliability by chaining through inexpensive local/prepaid LLMs (Ollama -> Codex) before escalating to more expensive APIs (Gemini/GPT-4o).
0cb3cb0bd08cef80cd51515df9ae473e6b3a0414.rejected: The previous model-specific routing directives (`/model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, etc.) and the associated complex subscription-based model switching logic.

00f8396d59e33c565a217d78a6f967c547d36556.title: Standardized Routing Protocol and Fixed MCP Registration Scope
00f8396d59e33c565a217d78a6f967c547d36556.rationale: Two major architectural decisions were implemented: 1) The tool's core communication protocol (the routing directive format) was standardized from an informal '⚡ ROUTE→' to a mandatory '⚡ MANDATORY ROUTE:' format. This prevents LLMs from ignoring critical tool calls. 2) The installation process was fixed to ensure the Multi-LLM MCP server registers in *both* the Code Desktop location and the Code CLI location, guaranteeing tool visibility across deployment types.

c51438b6c815b7a8b30f9f66f98f13045737409b.title: Implement headless mode and dynamic banner for Docker/CI Agents
c51438b6c815b7a8b30f9f66f98f13045737409b.rationale: The primary goal is to enable reliable, non-interactive deployment in containerized environments (Docker/CI/K8s agents). Traditional authentication methods relying on local OAuth or Keychain services fail or are unsuitable in these stateless, ephemeral environments. This required implementing a dedicated `--headless` mode and ensuring the startup banner and initialization logic dynamically adjust to API-key or subscription modes to avoid misleading log warnings and maintain service continuity.

5795334fe61bf1c72de95cfa546a8d656b5bd239.title: inline OAuth refresh prevents session 100% exhaustion
5795334fe61bf1c72de95cfa546a8d656b5bd239.rationale: The previous usage monitoring failed when usage.json was stale (>30 min) and resource pressure was high (≥70%). Stale data caused the system to underestimate true consumption, leading to catastrophic under-routing and eventual session exhaustion. The fix implements an inline OAuth refresh mechanism to ensure that the core routing decision process uses the freshest possible resource pressure data, maintaining system stability.

cc90433ab00398a635cec633e2594eea6139e340.title: Integrate dedicated platform support for Claw-Code Environment
cc90433ab00398a635cec633e2594eea6139e340.rationale: The system needed to add dedicated, context-aware hook logic to support the specific environment of 'claw-code'. This environment operates differently from standard integrations (e.g., it does not use Anthropic OAuth; calls are paid API calls), requiring specialized installation hooks and CLI flagging to ensure correct functionality.

2bd789c6ee8356d6e7186b87b3ae6d40f9bbc28e.title: Implement user evangelism hooks: shareable savings card and one-time star CTA
2bd789c6ee8356d6e7186b87b3ae6d40f9bbc28e.rationale: The goal is to move beyond utility and incorporate a 'growth' mechanic. By making the accumulated savings quantifiable and easily shareable (via a pre-filled tweet/clipboard card), the product aims to drive organic adoption and evangelism from satisfied users. The one-time CTA is a nuanced design choice to avoid annoying the user while maximizing impact.

f1e2864b5ad4765b5b51f0448e522ab241588327.title: Granular call breakdown in status bar (sub/free/paid)
f1e2864b5ad4765b5b51f0448e522ab241588327.rationale: To provide users with significantly better visibility into cost savings by differentiating between the type of LLM call (subscription, free provider, or paid provider). This improves user understanding of cost attribution.

436fa8086a07a28c82d86168baa87bb31570fe6b.title: Separate free-model savings in session-end summary
436fa8086a07a28c82d86168baa87bb31570fe6b.rationale: The cost summary calculation was updated to provide better cost transparency by explicitly separating and quantifying savings achieved using 'free-model' providers (Ollama, Codex) from paid subscriptions, improving user understanding of total cost savings.

6a82d4cf0a776dea3d2ea4fb360b3d4b9adebba2.title: Implement Filesystem Reasoning Tools and detailed Free-model Cost Savings tracking
6a82d4cf0a776dea3d2ea4fb360b3d4b9adebba2.rationale: The addition of filesystem tools (`llm_fs_*`) drastically increases the system's actionable complexity, allowing LLMs to perform real-world actions (find, rename, bulk edit). The cost savings tracking adds critical transparency, quantifying the value derived from using free/cheap models (Ollama/Codex) versus paid APIs. These features extend the core functionality of the router and require careful handling.

f8320eda833992f741ce3a852f1ec554c632d96c.title: Enhance dashboard metrics calculation and implement runtime version reporting
f8320eda833992f741ce3a852f1ec554c632d96c.rationale: To improve data fidelity and ensure the single source of truth for core metrics. Savings calculations were refactored to pull data directly from the granular `usage` table, eliminating reliance on a potentially stale or separate `savings_stats` table. Additionally, runtime version tracking was implemented on the dashboard for better operational visibility.

e1a1379b49c11ff82f9a83abf7dc652444eddc30.title: Animated SVG Demo in README
e1a1379b49c11ff82f9a83abf7dc652444eddc30.rationale: Replaced a static PNG demo image with a dynamically generated, animated SVG demonstration to improve the visual quality and perceived interactivity of the product showcase in the README.

1ab69188797b6fd74ab5afd7c44ffaaa4877b50f.title: Implement complete local state purging and real routing history demo
1ab69188797b6fd74ab5afd7c44ffaaa4877b50f.rationale: The decision was made to provide users with a definitive, irreversible method to wipe the entire local application state (database, credentials, logs) via 'uninstall --purge'. This improves data lifecycle management and user control. The added real routing history also significantly increases the utility and complexity of the demo.

e9efc08a5757c87e684a8aad8d3942207c3866f2.title: Deployment adaptation: Use environment variables for Port/Host binding
e9efc08a5757c87e684a8aad8d3942207c3866f2.rationale: The SSE server's binding logic was updated to prioritize reading the necessary network parameters (PORT and HOST) from environment variables ($PORT, $HOST). This change decouples the application from specific CLI argument formats and ensures compatibility with modern Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) environments (e.g., Railway, Render).

8f80c855c27c5b82146cf971bfbb949f59985321.title: Implement savings dashboard, update CLI, and improve cross-platform compatibility
8f80c855c27c5b82146cf971bfbb949f59985321.rationale: The addition of the savings dashboard provides users with critical visibility into cumulative costs vs. potential savings, enhancing user trust and usage monitoring. Furthermore, updating the compatibility layer to handle OS differences (like skipping `dst.chmod(0o755)` on Windows and using `sys.executable`) ensures the tool functions robustly across various environments (venv, pipx, etc.).

bfab03f63e6d8a05f193695660ac0899224e1421.title: fix(v1.3.8): cleaner demo GIF — no username path, no wrapping
bfab03f63e6d8a05f193695660ac0899224e1421.rationale: N/A (This was a cleanup and documentation update. The changes standardized demo paths by removing hardcoded user paths and updated model names for better readability, which improves usability but does not represent a core architectural shift.)

9ca4918aff0110ac2e6998f13f81347642612fcd.title: Implement actionable and structured authentication error handling
9ca4918aff0110ac2e6998f13f81347642612fcd.rationale: The previous error handling surfaced raw, cryptic exception text, making debugging difficult for users. This update centralizes authentication error detection (401/unauthorized) and provides specific, provider-aware hints (including the exact required environment variable) to greatly improve the developer experience and time-to-fix.

43f29426cbe91d6bbfef6548c7d154936e27d39e.title: Implement Deep Reasoning Tier, Setup Wizard, and Demo Command
43f29426cbe91d6bbfef6548c7d154936e27d39e.rationale: The addition of a 'deep_reasoning' complexity tier fundamentally expands the router's capability to handle highly advanced tasks (formal proofs, first-principles derivation), requiring special handling in the classification and routing layers. Furthermore, the interactive setup wizard standardizes initial configuration, and the demo command improves transparency regarding routing decisions.

2508169464ecd25a1e2bd014d349d9f00d2b27c6.title: Enhance diagnostics and standardize multi-IDE integration patterns
2508169464ecd25a1e2bd014d349d9f00d2b27c6.rationale: The primary goals were to significantly improve the developer experience and product reliability by adding a comprehensive 'doctor' health check command. Secondly, explicit documentation was needed to guide users through the unique Model Context Protocol (MCP) registration patterns required by popular IDEs (Cursor, Windsurf, Zed), ensuring the router can be correctly implemented across varied developer ecosystems.

41960b46062de6a8d383cb07929b9df589476b39.title: Enhance Visibility, Distribution, and User Trust through Status and Smithery Integration
41960b46062de6a8d383cb07929b9df589476b39.rationale: The architectural focus shifted to improved user experience, transparency, and ease of adoption. Visible routing indicators were added to make the complex process observable. The 'Shareable savings' feature proves immediate value and cost efficiency. Integrating with Smithery and standardizing the quick-start guide (zero-config) lowers the barrier to entry for new users, increasing adoption.

318cf5698f8d6b7a7cb79c9ac25c134e0a11f31b.title: Implement deep integration validation with Claude Desktop
318cf5698f8d6b7a7cb79c9ac25c134e0a11f31b.rationale: The setup process was enhanced to provide deep runtime validation. This change automatically checks if the 'llm-router' is correctly registered within the Claude Desktop configuration file and validates user-provided API keys, drastically improving the user onboarding and setup experience.

5eb0d12fd058444befe2878c96475b51a7ec26f0.title: Stitch Liquid Glass redesign — 4-tab SPA with real data
5eb0d12fd058444befe2878c96475b51a7ec26f0.rationale: Complete overhaul of the dashboard's front-end architecture and design system. The change moves from a previous 'Liquid Glass' design to 'The Ethereal Engine,' adopting a new color palette, font stack (adding JetBrains Mono), and significantly improving data sanitization and XSS protection by ensuring all rendered database values are HTML-escaped.

e584d609e32883220ca9ee8fcea89cc8f57e5ebd.title: Implement centralized, persistent usage tracking for LLM calls
e584d609e32883220ca9ee8fcea89cc8f57e5ebd.rationale: To provide observability for the dashboard, tracking all LLM model usage (tokens, model name, latency) in a centralized database, regardless of whether the underlying cost is flat-rate (subscription). This ensures metric availability for future savings estimation and usage analysis.

8b1e4889bd8faa860c8771d7050b40d47d567d17.title: Implement 'Liquid Glass' Dark Theme Design System
8b1e4889bd8faa860c8771d7050b40d47d567d17.rationale: To provide a modern, visually rich, and cohesive user experience (UX) by adopting a 'Liquid Glass' dark theme inspired by modern design systems (e.g., Google Stitch). This standardizes colors, typography (Inter, Space Grotesk), and general aesthetic components (using CSS variables) across the entire dashboard.

814def6f8b0f858d45b85eb32ae858060167c600.title: Implement comprehensive observability, cost control, and deduplication caching
814def6f8b0f858d45b85eb32ae858060167c600.rationale: The core decision is to introduce financial guardrails (daily spending caps) and efficiency mechanisms (prompt and semantic caching) to manage the growing operational costs associated with LLM API usage. The web dashboard provides necessary visibility into usage and cost trends.

ba423f7f02325c09779db57c4c07226fa8e10c64.title: Expand Codex utilization across all eligible task types
ba423f7f02325c09779db57c4c07226fa8e10c64.rationale: The routing logic was expanded to include `TaskType.QUERY` in the list of tasks eligible for Codex injection, moving from a limited set (CODE, ANALYZE, GENERATE) to a broader scope. This standardizes the prioritization principle (free local → free prepaid → paid per-call) across more use cases, ensuring that the relatively 'free' Codex capacity is utilized whenever possible, not just for code generation.

b9af7b74ce60cddbbba17706ea64b1d87732260b.title: Decomposition of Monolith and Introduction of Standardized State Management
b9af7b74ce60cddbbba17706ea64b1d87732260b.rationale: The original `server.py` was a 2,328-line monolith, making it difficult to maintain, extend, and test. The decision was made to decompose this into 8 smaller, focused tools/modules and extract all shared, mutable state into a dedicated `state.py` module. This promotes extensibility, improves testability, and isolates state changes, enabling stateless scaling for the LLM routing service.
b9af7b74ce60cddbbba17706ea64b1d87732260b.rejected: The monolithic structure of `server.py` was rejected in favor of a modular, composer-based architecture.

030f625025ee667c56f062de7d030c36aacd23fb.title: Decompose monolithic server.py into structured tool modules
030f625025ee667c56f062de7d030c36aacd23fb.rationale: The monolithic `server.py` file exceeded 2,300 lines, making it difficult to maintain, navigate, and test. This refactor decomposes the functionality into specialized, focused modules (e.g., `admin.py`, `codex.py`, `text.py`), adhering to the Single Responsibility Principle and improving the overall architectural modularity and separation of concerns.

8be2dfad62655d04b97ad663150326aa24b504fc.title: Revamp LLM Routing Strategy: Codex Prioritization and Capacity Optimization
8be2dfad62655d04b97ad663150326aa24b504fc.rationale: The routing logic was updated in two major ways: 1) Implemented dedicated plugin detection for Codex to ensure the highest fidelity routing path. 2) Optimized capacity triggering by refining the thresholds for using external/paid models. The primary goal is to ensure cost efficiency while maintaining functionality, by correctly prioritizing pre-paid/subscription capacity.
8be2dfad62655d04b97ad663150326aa24b504fc.rejected: Reverted the 'session ≥ 85%' capacity threshold rule. This rule was rejected because at 85% usage, there is still sufficient Haiku subscription capacity remaining (15%), which violates the architectural principle of preferring already-paid capacity before triggering expensive external routing.

ea22ac8bd28dc35bd0a243b4079f7499db1731f0.title: Implement LLM Cost Monitoring and Routing Feedback Loop
ea22ac8bd28dc35bd0a243b4079f7499db1731f0.rationale: The integration of daily spend limits and alerts is necessary for financial governance regarding external LLM API costs. Furthermore, implementing a user feedback loop (thumbs up/down) allows the router to continuously learn and improve its decision-making process, moving toward a quality-aware routing system.

6177c5e456bcdfbf8fc74c5d0497ed34479a965c.title: Implement separate tracking for internal CC subscription model usage
6177c5e456bcdfbf8fc74c5d0497ed34479a965c.rationale: To accurately report and distinguish usage derived from internal subscription hints (CC-MODE) from external API calls. This separation is necessary for improved cost visibility and a comprehensive session summary.

d09604b471e5dc794b279f73d02f33ffc9af2573.title: Implement CC subscription delta tracking for session summary
d09604b471e5dc794b279f73d02f33ffc9af2573.rationale: To provide a unified and accurate session summary by tracking the delta usage (change) of the Claude Code (CC) subscription percentage between the start and end of a session, improving usage visibility for the user.

5b75da5d09932f4f9560f0e0c0b458cbd9ca3505.title: Live OAuth Refresh for Usage Tracking
5b75da5d09932f4f9560f0e0c0b458cbd9ca3505.rationale: Replaced stale file usage tracking with live API calls (OAuth) to fetch fresh, accurate subscription usage data for both Claude and external services, ensuring real-time cost awareness at session end. This significantly increases data reliability for cost tracking.

15cf0936459a06ee08dc299d1144f7bd0040524b.title: Implement Subscription-Aware Routing and Ollama Health Probe
15cf0936459a06ee08dc299d1144f7bd0040524b.rationale: Two major architectural improvements were implemented: 1) Proactive and cached health checking for the local Ollama service to increase reliability and reduce network calls. 2) Implementing subscription-aware routing logic to detect when the internal Claude Code quota has sufficient headroom, preventing costly external API calls and optimizing usage costs.

de1d44331671e63e08d56458b65ee65e79af8847.title: Externalize and formalize Ollama startup logic
de1d44331671e63e08d56458b65ee65e79af8847.rationale: The core Python hook logic for starting the local LLM service (Ollama) was refactored to delegate the startup process to a dedicated shell script. This decision improves modularity, centralizes complex system process management (timeouts, failure modes, logging) outside of Python, and increases the reliability of the service initialization process.

6e4788d925b96be36d847e058dbc9a11a4445b8e.title: Implement proactive service health checks and usage refresh at session start
6e4788d925b96be36d847e058dbc9a11a4445b8e.rationale: The router needs to ensure that required external dependencies (Ollama) are running and available, and that the current usage metrics for paid services (Claude) are up-to-date upon session initialization. This transition moves resource management from reactive to proactive.

1a9a840337dfc6c48fd511acef4d92a2f31bf44f.title: Implement real-time resource tracking and enhanced session summary
1a9a840337dfc6c48fd511acef4d92a2f31bf44f.rationale: To provide users with high visibility into their computational resource usage. The new status bar displays live usage of Claude Code subscription credits (session %, weekly %, sonnet %), while the end-of-session hook updates the summary to include this credit status and explicitly reports percentage savings vs. the Sonnet baseline.

78c06bbec17ebdd6377720291184e15f6f9ab264.title: Roadmap Update and Completion of Core Architectural Features (v1.0.0)
78c06bbec17ebdd6377720291184e15f6f9ab264.rationale: The system transitioned from an experimental proof-of-concept to a robust, production-grade routing layer (v1.0.0). Key decisions formalized were optimizing cost-efficiency (e.g., quality-cost tier sorting, DeepSeek preference), ensuring high availability via comprehensive routing rules, and implementing sophisticated memory management (cross-session context injection) to improve user experience and reliability.

4d6b39434e9f8f58bd884e253d1e3e4526074125.title: Unify LLM Routing Profile Generation
4d6b39434e9f8f58bd884e253d1e3e4526074125.rationale: The previous implementation allowed `llm_route` and `llm_classify` to derive routing profiles based on different inputs (complexity vs. budget pressure), leading to non-deterministic routing. Unification ensures that all tools use a shared, consistent decision path for profile generation, eliminating ambiguity.

9a3252ddcb9fe87b0729b6c6faf99ac975659078.title: Implement Claude Code Subscription Mode
9a3252ddcb9fe87b0729b6c6faf99ac975659078.rationale: To allow users running the LLM Router inside the Claude Code environment to benefit from their Pro/Max subscription quota (zero API cost) without triggering accidental double-billing. This requires explicitly filtering out the 'anthropic' provider from API routing chains when the subscription flag is set.

7de93ecb4f250a905006f095d01d236567b08350.title: Improve LLM Routing Resilience and Graceful Degradation
7de93ecb4f250a905006f095d01d236567b08350.rationale: The system was updated to gracefully handle the unavailability or misconfiguration of external LLM services (Ollama and Perplexity) rather than failing hard. This involves checking Ollama connectivity upfront, providing clear fallbacks (e.g., using Gemini Flash), and notifying the user if necessary keys or models are missing, improving overall operational reliability.

a66a6118cc6125ebb3009fa067697085879880df.title: Switch usage data fetching from AppleScript injection to OAuth API
a66a6118cc6125ebb3009fa067697085879880df.rationale: The mechanism for retrieving usage data was migrated from a brittle, platform-specific AppleScript injection (which was hard to maintain) to using the standardized Claude Code OAuth usage API. This ensures greater reliability and portability across different client environments.

6318e867d4ae8f148ca1bb6d752ab5a9ce9a3606.title: Implement SubagentStart hook for dynamic routing context injection
6318e867d4ae8f148ca1bb6d752ab5a9ce9a3606.rationale: The routing mechanism is decoupled from simple logic and now dynamically adapts based on real-time 'subscription pressure' (usage of Claude APIs). This ensures that when primary LLM resources are nearing capacity (HIGH/CRITICAL pressure), agents are automatically rerouted to external/fallback services to maintain operational continuity and prevent unexpected cost overruns.

819dcbb6dd9a3edb2bf24989551b51a8a9341fec.title: Enforce structured routing and integrate native usage tracking for external LLMs
819dcbb6dd9a3edb2bf24989551b51a8a9341fec.rationale: Two major changes: 1) The auto-route hook is updated to eliminate silent passthrough, forcing the LLM to use a designated tool for 'moderate' complexity tasks instead of self-answering, ensuring 100% routing coverage. 2) New logic is added to retrieve external LLM (Claude) usage data by using AppleScript injection into the browser, avoiding the overhead and complexity of Playwright and browser locking.

f04669948e25101679fa5af9233c249c1379947e.title: Implement granular, multi-metric quota tracking and adaptive LLM routing logic
f04669948e25101679fa5af9233c249c1379947e.rationale: The previous single-pressure quota tracking was too simplistic for managing diverse LLM resources. This update replaces it with separate tracking for session, sonnet, and weekly usage. This granularity allows the router to make highly nuanced, budget-aware routing decisions and implement robust failover mechanisms, thereby preventing unexpected cost overruns or rate limiting.

79466bd01da33f8ade1b322eec58dc26d4f06a1d.title: fix(v0.9.1): version bump, pressure fallback, validation, error hints, CODEX_PATH
79466bd01da33f8ade1b322eec58dc26d4f06a1d.rationale: This commit is primarily a version bump and maintenance release. The changes implement robustness improvements (validation, error handling, path management for CODEX) and update configuration files to improve project structure and tooling compatibility rather than introducing a new core architectural paradigm.

a30a6879cad57d3ca871b66fd1edbaa77508e071.title: Wire complexity to profile as foundational routing rule
a30a6879cad57d3ca871b66fd1edbaa77508e071.rationale: Standardizes the LLM call process by making task complexity (simple, moderate, complex) the single source of truth for determining the required model profile (BUDGET, BALANCED, PREMIUM). This ensures that the cost/capability tier automatically aligns with the task difficulty, greatly improving consistency and manageability.

71d590dc29cc10df6b091509a21ce9104bd55ed0.title: Implemented controlled usage budgets and full cost savings tracking
71d590dc29cc10df6b091509a21ce9104bd55ed0.rationale: The system was transitioned from unlimited, uncontrolled usage (default budgets of 0) to finite, controlled budgets (e.g., $20 monthly cap, 500k tokens/day). Furthermore, the core logic in `cost.py` was upgraded to implement a sophisticated calculation that tracks and quantifies 'routing savings' by comparing actual incurred costs against a standardized baseline (Sonnet 4.6 pricing).

f037530dd2a0d4c359c4f1f7c4cf50f822e5e8d8.title: Implement pressure-aware routing and formal cost tracking
f037530dd2a0d4c359c4f1f7c4cf50f822e5e8d8.rationale: The architecture is upgraded to include two key features: 1) Quota/Resource Awareness: The router now reads a usage JSON file to detect pressure on premium models (like Claude), dynamically adjusting routing to use less costly/more available models when quotas are low. 2) Financial Transparency: Cost tracking is moved to a structured SQLite database, establishing a formal baseline (Sonnet 4.6) against which all savings can be accurately measured, providing real business value reporting.

995eddb8242cd068013693b14096466e1013d72f.title: Implement PreToolUse Hook for Cost-Aware Subagent Routing
995eddb8242cd068013693b14096466e1013d72f.rationale: The primary goal is to enforce strict cost controls by intercepting subagent spawning. The system analyzes agent intent (retrieval vs. reasoning) and routes tasks accordingly. Reasoning tasks are funneled to a more cost-effective MCP tool rather than directly using expensive models like Opus, mitigating high operational costs and enabling scalable usage based on real-time quota pressure.

f79cf86894593773120f860523c0b0b908f8ecfd.title: Architectural overhaul of asynchronous model routing and penalty calculation
f79cf86894593773120f860523c0b0b908f8ecfd.rationale: The previous model routing system failed to correctly calculate model penalties (based on failure rates and latency stats) in production asynchronous contexts due to deadlocks and event loop detection errors. This fix implements a robust, pre-fetching pattern: all necessary operational metrics are now gathered asynchronously using asyncio.gather() *before* model chain construction. These results are then passed down as optional parameters, ensuring the self-learning feedback system remains accurate and functional in a highly concurrent production environment. Additionally, core business logic for profile-specific routing (BUDGET and RESEARCH) was corrected to reliably manage model quota pressure.

dee75eca0cd2f7c01489b2aff00863a782e6672d.title: Implementing advanced routing strategies and code-edit tooling
dee75eca0cd2f7c01489b2aff00863a782e6672d.rationale: Multiple critical architectural improvements were implemented to improve cost efficiency, system robustness, and stability. Key improvements include: 1) Introducing `llm_edit` to route mechanical code-editing reasoning to a cheap model, significantly reducing expensive calls to flagship models (e.g., Opus). 2) Implementing availability-aware routing, which incorporates P95 latency into the benchmark scoring, ensuring system performance degrades gracefully as external services struggle. 3) Setting cold-start defaults for Codex to prevent costly latency spikes and maintain predictable system behavior on fresh installs.

255c3e12b979a6858230440519aa6e2df43fb0bc.title: Implement Availability-Aware Routing and Latency Penalties
255c3e12b979a6858230440519aa6e2df43fb0bc.rationale: The original scoring mechanism only considered failure rates. By folding P50/P95 latency into the quality score, the system prioritizes not just capability, but actual real-world performance consistency, making routing decisions far more robust.

80d7fa11935a32efdbe824149741a00f890f3bc2.title: Introduce Dedicated Code-Edit MCP Tool Workflow (llm_edit)
80d7fa11935a32efdbe824149741a00f890f3bc2.rationale: To reduce operational cost and complexity when editing code, the system now offloads the creative/reasoning step (determining file/old/new strings) to a cheaper, dedicated LLM. Claude is reserved for mechanical, execution-only application of the instructions, thereby optimizing the entire editing pipeline.

eea6aa772ac0f8ef0f4da98e07c254f96b124c80.title: Establish Cold-Start Latency Defaults for Models
eea6aa772ac0f8ef0f4da98e07c254f96b124c80.rationale: Models that have no historical routing data (cold-start) must be assigned pessimistic P95 latency defaults (e.g., 60s or 90s) to prevent them from being artificially prioritized in routing decisions until sufficient usage history is available.

78c5b21a48a60bfd4180aed9d2ba3399b46ea56b.title: Implement Quality-Cost Aware LLM Routing & Subscription Mode
78c5b21a48a60bfd4180aed9d2ba3399b46ea56b.rationale: The system was upgraded to dynamically route tasks based on a complex interplay of economic cost and model quality. Key decisions include: 1) Implementing a 'Subscription Mode' to prevent double-billing and preserve API quotas when using paid services. 2) Establishing a formal 'Quality-Cost' sorting heuristic (cost-per-token blended analysis) to ensure the cheapest model that meets the quality requirement is always chosen. 3) Integrating high-performance, cost-effective models like DeepSeek-Reasoner into core chains to maintain performance at high utilization.

eca0e75b4273d6561bd675f8e42889067dbd9396.title: Implement subscription-aware and pressure-aware LLM routing
eca0e75b4273d6561bd675f8e42889067dbd9396.rationale: The system was upgraded to dynamically monitor and adapt model routing chains based on live Claude Pro/Max subscription usage ('quota pressure'). This ensures that the most expensive, high-quality models are prioritized until quota is nearly exhausted (85% usage), after which the router automatically falls back to cheaper, open-source, or lower-tier models to maintain operational continuity and manage costs effectively.

d6f465bdf4a02b90ebd4dc77a46495b94150fc3e.title: Implementing Structured Hooks and Mandating Prompt Directives for Routing Enforcement
d6f465bdf4a02b90ebd4dc77a46495b94150fc3e.rationale: The primary goal was to enforce routing mandates and improve system observability. The directive was moved from a soft, multi-line hint to a high-density, mandatory single-line format to minimize LLM deviation. New session start/end hooks were added to track operational metrics (tool usage, missed routing instances, estimated cost savings) necessary for measuring and optimizing the routing layer's efficiency.

7e18b4e6388e89b0fb62c4c357fa642236af1e8a.title: Enforce routing as hard constraint with MANDATORY ROUTE directive
7e18b4e6388e89b0fb62c4c357fa642236af1e8a.rationale: The previous soft routing hints were insufficient for cost control. The decision elevates routing hints to a mandatory, hard constraint (⚡ MANDATORY ROUTE:). This is necessary to strictly enforce the use of cheaper models (Gemini Flash, Haiku) and prevent costly hallucinations or over-processing by the high-cost Opus model, by prohibiting actions like subagents, WebSearch, or self-answering when a route is given.

dc0f442c4fbaaf63c14c2f669811c0e35062ba75.title: Implement Benchmark-Driven Model Routing Transparency
dc0f442c4fbaaf63c14c2f669811c0e35062ba75.rationale: To establish transparency and confidence in the model selection process. By documenting how models are quantitatively ranked (based on performance, cost, and task type), the team can educate users on why certain models are recommended for specific tasks, thereby setting clear expectations for system performance and cost.

c8c9ad1d906dfff1b7538f512355feec791f24bc.title: Multi-stage prompt classification and self-updating hooks
c8c9ad1d906dfff1b7538f512355feec791f24bc.rationale: To significantly improve cost efficiency and reduce latency by implementing a multi-stage prompt classification funnel. Simple tasks are now routed through free/local methods (Heuristics -> Ollama) before escalating to paid APIs (Gemini/GPT-4o-mini), ensuring high-cost models are only used when necessary.

68e50b26910000090ae10c2e6a56833888189e27.title: Refactor and stabilize LLM Benchmark Sources and Model Aliasing
68e50b26910000090ae10c2e6a56833888189e27.rationale: Migrated benchmark data fetching from deprecated/general sources (like LMSYS) to specialized, structured, and current sources (e.g., Arena Hard Auto CSV, v2 leaderboards) to ensure benchmark reliability and accuracy. Also significantly refactored and expanded the model aliasing map to support versioning and new frontier models correctly.

bc0ff924352acbb8e7850b5ad70eb07a0764de21.title: Implement benchmark-driven routing table with weekly auto-update
bc0ff924352acbb8e7850b5ad70eb07a0764de21.rationale: To ensure the routing logic always uses the most up-to-date, performance-validated model pairings available in the LLM ecosystem, decoupling the core router from manually maintained, potentially stale configuration rules.

1e8ecb492259b5e5789e854565b1d6f43ebb6b58.title: Restrict Ollama local model usage exclusively to the budget tier
1e8ecb492259b5e5789e854565b1d6f43ebb6b58.rationale: The system determined that local Ollama models are only suitable for simple, low-complexity tasks (budget tier). They lack the capability required for heavy-weight reasoning, complex architecture decisions, or multi-step analysis, necessitating the restriction of their use to the budget routing chain, while reserving balanced and premium tasks for cloud providers.

5a929669ddd8ffe23c77edda497d3bf5f4697b84.title: Support for local model execution via Ollama and cost-optimization explainer.
5a929669ddd8ffe23c77edda497d3bf5f4697b84.rationale: The core rationale is cost reduction, privacy, and enhancing local development capability. By adding Ollama support, simple tasks can be routed to free local models, dramatically cutting cloud API costs (up to 85%). This shift makes the system more private (no data leaves the machine) and sustainable regarding API budgets.

e6bb8597ea718b38ce1bdcc03b4d7e81b87e9a86.title: Improve model routing and result representation for better observability
e6bb8597ea718b38ce1bdcc03b4d7e81b87e9a86.rationale: Standardized and cleaned up the logging and result reporting mechanism. The goal was to make the output (both console logs and structured responses) more readable, informative, and visually consistent by utilizing emojis and grouping key metrics (tokens, cost, latency) clearly.

3876778fbc3dc56400e0571aecf97bbb2a21e9f2.title: Implement persistent session context injection and auto-update rules
3876778fbc3dc56400e0571aecf97bbb2a21e9f2.rationale: To provide external Large Language Models (LLMs) with complete conversation history and persistent cross-session context. This solves the problem of models losing context between interactions by introducing a two-layer system: a temporary buffer (current session) and persistent SQLite summaries (previous sessions). It also increases reliability by implementing auto-update checks for routing rules, ensuring users receive the latest model logic upon package upgrade.

77b7be1d01c59d03ce89947599a6f94d356c8ba2.title: Major feature release v0.4.0: Context Compaction, Global Hooks, and Media Support
77b7be1d01c59d03ce89947599a6f94d356c8ba2.rationale: The system is significantly enhanced across several fronts: 1) **Efficiency/Cost:** Implementing structural context compaction reduces token usage. 2) **Observability:** Introducing quality logging tracks the full routing lifecycle for better auditing and analysis. 3) **Capability:** Adding support for multimodal outputs (Gemini Imagen/Veo) expands the toolset. 4) **Persistence:** Global hooks and savings logging ensure consistent, system-wide enforcement of rules and resource tracking.

87b9d4072bd1dd96683661a5adb1dba602e43b5e.title: Multi-layer auto-classification with Ollama + API fallback chain
87b9d4072bd1dd96683661a5adb1dba602e43b5e.rationale: Replaced simple first-match regex classification with a sophisticated, multi-stage scoring pipeline. This ensures robust and reliable routing by prioritizing local, free, and fast mechanisms (skips, heuristics, Ollama) before falling back to costly cloud APIs (GPT-4o-mini/Gemini Flash), maximizing both efficiency and accuracy.

127b35616792e4bc6daecec460d1dfe4e53c381d.title: Global routing overhaul: Rewriting the auto-route hook to classify all user prompts.
127b35616792e4bc6daecec460d1dfe4e53c381d.rationale: The previous system limited routing to specific task domains (e.g., only research). This change makes the auto-route hook classify ALL user prompts and inject routing hints. This significantly increases coverage and aims for better user experience by ensuring that any complex or ambiguous prompt is routed to the most appropriate LLM tool, rather than failing or being miscategorized.

9baa33d10cc43c0886245755b7efe232b00a6adf.title: Implement robust prompt handling layer: Caching, Auto-Routing, Streaming, and Rate Limiting
9baa33d10cc43c0886245755b7efe232b00a6adf.rationale: To improve overall system reliability, performance, and resource efficiency. Auto-routing redirects prompts to the most appropriate LLM tool pre-emptively (saving costly round-trips). Caching prevents reprocessing identical requests. Rate limiting and streaming enhance stability and user experience under load.

2a74c0199921588ce80705c0503880ed1e523a04.title: Resolve Ruff lint errors (Unused imports, unused vars)
2a74c0199921588ce80705c0503880ed1e523a04.rationale: This commit consists solely of linting fixes (unused imports, unused variables, semicolons) and does not represent an architectural or design decision.

24860296eefca679c79dff791021a217bd59a736.title: Implement Complexity-First Routing and Unified Provider Monitoring
24860296eefca679c79dff791021a217bd59a736.rationale: The system is upgrading from simple budget-based downshifting to a 'Complexity-first' routing mechanism. This enhances resilience and intelligence by matching the task complexity (S/M/C) to the optimal model (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) first, and using budget monitoring (Claude/Codex/API spend) as a secondary safety net. This allows for better scalability and cost prediction.

9bc3701f3d3eb2f1d6835985dd52daafaed3c28d.title: Implement smart, budget-aware LLM routing based on complexity classification
9bc3701f3d3eb2f1d6835985dd52daafaed3c28d.rationale: To move beyond simple routing, the system now uses an intelligent classifier to determine the required complexity of a task and selects the optimal LLM (from Claude Code's internal models or external APIs). Crucially, it incorporates a 'Progressive Budget Downshift' mechanism, automatically switching to cheaper models (e.g., Opus -> Sonnet -> Haiku) as the user approaches their allocated daily token budget, thus optimizing cost and ensuring reliability.

c24a020171f4dd2f83d2d1024c68358d406378b0.title: Restructure components to follow installable plugin convention
c24a020171f4dd2f83d2d1024c68358d406378b0.rationale: To align the project structure and plugin components with the official 'installable Claude Code plugin' convention, moving agents and skills from an internal subdirectory to the repo root. This improves discoverability and deployment flow.

8bd834eae6554d900b34f41628278e2a4283aef8.title: Implement Freemium Gating, Budget Enforcement, and Advanced Tooling
8bd834eae6554d900b34f41628278e2a4283aef8.rationale: The core business model requires revenue gating and predictable cost management. We implemented freemium limitations and budget enforcement (cost tracking). Architecturally, this required expanding the tool registry to include media generation, orchestration pipelines, and dedicated management tools, significantly increasing the system's capability and complexity.

51f3e3c88b522a1e5ae6beb5dfa2975fefc09207.title: Enhanced multi-modal and provider orchestration capabilities
51f3e3c88b522a1e5ae6beb5dfa2975fefc09207.rationale: To achieve maximum vendor neutrality and flexibility, the router was expanded from supporting a limited number of providers to supporting a comprehensive suite of 20+ text LLMs and multiple media generation APIs. This dynamic selection capability allows the system to automatically choose the optimal provider based on cost, performance, or required modality, fundamentally improving system resilience and expanding the feature set.
